prerequisites: A Jira Cloud site, identified by its hostname (the domain config field), for example yourcompany.atlassian.net. Provide the bare hostname without the https:// prefix or any path.
An authentication credential matching one of the supported methods: an Atlassian account email plus API token, an OAuth 2.0 (3LO) app, or an Atlassian service account API token.
Jira permissions sufficient to read the projects and data you intend to sync.
Optionally, the project keys to sync (the projects config field). Leave empty to replicate all accessible projects.
Optionally, a start_date in UTC (format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) to bound the sync window for incremental streams such as issues, comments, worklogs, and changelogs.

authMethods.name: API token (Basic auth)
authMethods.summary: Uses the credentials.api_token secret together with your Atlassian account email and the Jira site domain to authenticate via HTTP Basic auth. Create the token at id.atlassian.com under Security > API tokens; a token works for the REST API with or without scopes. The token is shown once, so save it securely.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (3LO)
authMethods.summary: Uses credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and credentials.refresh_token. Register an OAuth 2.0 (3LO) app in the Atlassian Developer Console to obtain the client ID and secret, configure the callback URL and Jira scopes, then complete the authorization flow to obtain tokens. Authorizes access on a user's behalf.

authMethods.name: Service account API token
authMethods.summary: Uses the credentials.service_account_token secret. An organization admin creates an API token for an Atlassian service account in Atlassian Administration; service account tokens use scopes. Useful for unattended, non-personal automation access.

setupSteps.title: Determine your Jira domain
setupSteps.body: Set the domain field to your Jira Cloud site hostname, for example yourcompany.atlassian.net. Do not include https:// or any trailing path.

setupSteps.title: Choose an authentication method and obtain credentials
setupSteps.body: For the API token method, sign in at https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens, select Create API token, name it, optionally set an expiry, click Create, and copy the token immediately (it cannot be retrieved later). Use this token with your Atlassian account email. For OAuth 2.0, register a 3LO app in the Atlassian Developer Console to get the client ID and secret, set the callback URL and Jira scopes, and run the authorization flow to obtain a refresh token. For a service account, have an org admin create a scoped API token in Atlassian Administration.

setupSteps.title: Configure the credentials block
setupSteps.body: Populate the credentials config object with the secret(s) for your chosen method: api_token (plus email) for the API token method; client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token for OAuth 2.0; or service_account_token for a service account.

setupSteps.title: Scope the sync (optional)
setupSteps.body: Optionally set projects to a list of Jira project keys to limit the sync, and set start_date (UTC, YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) to bound incremental streams. Optional tuning fields include expand_issue_changelog, expand_issue_transition, issues_stream_expand_with, render_fields, lookback_window_minutes, and num_workers.

sources.title: Manage API tokens for your Atlassian account
ID Atlassian - Security - API Token Management
Basic auth for REST APIs - Atlassian Developer
Manage API tokens for service accounts - Atlassian Support
